title: Dry shampoo powder
subtitle: A loose-powder dry shampoo with arrowroot, kaolin clay, and lavender
excerpt: Arrowroot powder absorbs oil from the scalp and hair root without leaving a visible white residue. Kaolin clay adds absorption and a slight grip. Lavender essential oil adds fragrance and is compatible with all hair types. The powder applies with a brush or fingertips and is brushed through the hair.
sourceNotes: Dry shampoo formulation from Making Cosmetics guide (makingcosmetics.com, May 2026). Arrowroot and kaolin properties from supplier datasheets. Lavender EO usage rates from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale the batch proportionally. Store excess in an airtight container.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in any quantity and store in a small jar or a recycled spice shaker for easy dispensing.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed container away from moisture. This formula contains no water and no preservative is required. Keep the container dry: even a small amount of moisture will cause clumping.

glossaryTerms.definition: Ultra-fine starch ground from the arrowroot plant root. Absorbs oil and moisture without the astringency of clay. Gives a silky, non-sticky finish when applied to the scalp. Different from the thickening-agent arrowroot used in cooking.

content.text: Commercial dry shampoos use silica as the active absorber; this formula uses

combined with kaolin clay. The blend absorbs scalp oil and gives hair root a slightly matte, lifted appearance. Kaolin is the mildest of the cosmetic clays and is suitable for dry and sensitive scalps. For dark hair, add 1 to 2 teaspoons of cocoa powder to the mix to avoid visible residue at the roots.

Apply by tapping a small amount of powder onto the roots with a brush, fingertips, or by shaking from a spice shaker. Work in section by section and leave for 2 minutes to absorb before brushing through. Do not inhale the powder during application.

items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade arrowroot starch; the main absorber
Approximately 0.5 ml; add drop by drop and sift through the powder to distribute evenly

content.text: Weigh the arrowroot powder and kaolin clay into a bowl. Stir to combine. Add the lavender essential oil one or two drops at a time, rubbing each addition through the powder with your fingertips to prevent clumping. Sift through a fine-mesh sieve to break up any aggregates. Transfer to an airtight jar or a small spice shaker with a perforated lid.
12 months in a sealed, dry container. If the powder clumps, break it up with a fork and sift again. Keep away from the shower to avoid moisture entering the container.
